---
title: "Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes"
---

# Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes

Range: `1583-1645`.

## Completion

| Status   | Count |
| -------- | ----- |
| authored | 63    |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                  | Completion | Semantic        | Summary                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ----- | ------------------------------------- | ---------- | --------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1583  | [-able](#rune-1583)                   | authored   | generated_draft | `-able` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -able obligation named before work proceeds.                                         |
| 1584  | [-aware](#rune-1584)                  | authored   | reviewed        | Marks that something notices the surrounding condition it names.                                                                                                                                                                             |
| 1585  | [-by-design](#rune-1585)              | authored   | generated_draft | `-by-design` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -by-design obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 1586  | [-centric](#rune-1586)                | authored   | generated_draft | `-centric` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -centric obligation named before work proceeds.                                   |
| 1587  | [-complete](#rune-1587)               | authored   | generated_draft | `-complete` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -complete obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1588  | [-driven](#rune-1588)                 | authored   | reviewed        | Signals that behavior is organized around events or causes.                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| 1589  | [-first](#rune-1589)                  | authored   | reviewed        | Declares priority or design center: security-first, API-first, offline-first.                                                                                                                                                                |
| 1590  | [-free](#rune-1590)                   | authored   | generated_draft | `-free` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -free obligation named before work proceeds.                                         |
| 1591  | [-friendly](#rune-1591)               | authored   | generated_draft | `-friendly` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -friendly obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1592  | [-hard](#rune-1592)                   | authored   | generated_draft | `-hard` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -hard obligation named before work proceeds.                                         |
| 1593  | [-less](#rune-1593)                   | authored   | generated_draft | `-less` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -less obligation named before work proceeds.                                         |
| 1594  | [-local](#rune-1594)                  | authored   | generated_draft | `-local` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -local obligation named before work proceeds.                                       |
| 1595  | [-native](#rune-1595)                 | authored   | generated_draft | `-native` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -native obligation named before work proceeds.                                     |
| 1596  | [-of-record](#rune-1596)              | authored   | generated_draft | `-of-record` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -of-record obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 1597  | [-oriented](#rune-1597)               | authored   | generated_draft | `-oriented` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -oriented obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1598  | [-safe](#rune-1598)                   | authored   | reviewed        | Asserts a protected behavior such as memory-safe or thread-safe.                                                                                                                                                                             |
| 1599  | [-sensitive](#rune-1599)              | authored   | generated_draft | `-sensitive` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -sensitive obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 1600  | [-shaped](#rune-1600)                 | authored   | generated_draft | `-shaped` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -shaped obligation named before work proceeds.                                     |
| 1601  | [-soft](#rune-1601)                   | authored   | generated_draft | `-soft` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -soft obligation named before work proceeds.                                         |
| 1602  | [-tolerant](#rune-1602)               | authored   | generated_draft | `-tolerant` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -tolerant obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1603  | [-wide](#rune-1603)                   | authored   | generated_draft | `-wide` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -wide obligation named before work proceeds.                                         |
| 1604  | [actor model](#rune-1604)             | authored   | generated_draft | `actor model` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the actor model obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 1605  | [API-first](#rune-1605)               | authored   | reviewed        | Design the interface surface before the implementations behind it.                                                                                                                                                                           |
| 1606  | [append-only](#rune-1606)             | authored   | reviewed        | History grows by addition rather than mutation.                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| 1607  | [best-effort](#rune-1607)             | authored   | generated_draft | `best-effort` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the best-effort obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 1608  | [branch-free](#rune-1608)             | authored   | generated_draft | `branch-free` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the branch-free obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 1609  | [cloud-native](#rune-1609)            | authored   | generated_draft | `cloud-native` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the cloud-native obligation named before work proceeds.                           |
| 1610  | [command-query](#rune-1610)           | authored   | generated_draft | `command-query` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the command-query obligation named before work proceeds.                         |
| 1611  | [control plane {Rune}](#rune-1611)    | authored   | generated_draft | `control plane` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the control plane obligation named before work proceeds.       |
| 1612  | [copy-on-write {Rune}](#rune-1612)    | authored   | generated_draft | `copy-on-write` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the copy-on-write obligation named before work proceeds.       |
| 1613  | [crash-only](#rune-1613)              | authored   | reviewed        | Only live briefly, start fast, and recover by restart rather than repair.                                                                                                                                                                    |
| 1614  | [data plane {Rune}](#rune-1614)       | authored   | generated_draft | `data plane` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the data plane obligation named before work proceeds.             |
| 1615  | [data-driven](#rune-1615)             | authored   | generated_draft | `data-driven` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the data-driven obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 1616  | [default-deny](#rune-1616)            | authored   | reviewed        | Access is forbidden unless explicitly granted.                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| 1617  | [edge-native](#rune-1617)             | authored   | generated_draft | `edge-native` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the edge-native obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 1618  | [event-driven](#rune-1618)            | authored   | reviewed        | Action propagates from events rather than from direct polling or command loops.                                                                                                                                                              |
| 1619  | [eventually consistent](#rune-1619)   | authored   | generated_draft | `eventually consistent` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the eventually consistent obligation named before work proceeds.         |
| 1620  | [fail-fast](#rune-1620)               | authored   | reviewed        | Abort early when assumptions break instead of smearing damage forward.                                                                                                                                                                       |
| 1621  | [first-class](#rune-1621)             | authored   | generated_draft | `first-class` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the first-class obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 1622  | [hot path {Rune}](#rune-1622)         | authored   | generated_draft | `hot path` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the hot path obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 1623  | [infrastructure-as-code](#rune-1623)  | authored   | reviewed        | Operational change expressed as reviewable source.                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| 1624  | [least-authority](#rune-1624)         | authored   | generated_draft | `least-authority` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the least-authority obligation named before work proceeds.                     |
| 1625  | [lock-free {Rune}](#rune-1625)        | authored   | generated_draft | `lock-free` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the lock-free obligation named before work proceeds.               |
| 1626  | [machine-readable](#rune-1626)        | authored   | reviewed        | Structured so software, not only humans, can read it reliably.                                                                                                                                                                               |
| 1627  | [memory-safe {Rune}](#rune-1627)      | authored   | generated_draft | `memory-safe` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the memory-safe obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 1628  | [multi-tenant](#rune-1628)            | authored   | generated_draft | `multi-tenant` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the multi-tenant obligation named before work proceeds.                           |
| 1629  | [negative cache](#rune-1629)          | authored   | generated_draft | `negative cache` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the negative cache obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 1630  | [object-capability](#rune-1630)       | authored   | generated_draft | `object-capability` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the object-capability obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 1631  | [offline-first {Rune}](#rune-1631)    | authored   | reviewed        | Designed to keep working with poor or absent connectivity.                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| 1632  | [policy-as-code](#rune-1632)          | authored   | reviewed        | Policy declared as versioned, testable code.                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 1633  | [queue-backed](#rune-1633)            | authored   | generated_draft | `queue-backed` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the queue-backed obligation named before work proceeds.                           |
| 1634  | [read path](#rune-1634)               | authored   | generated_draft | `read path` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the read path obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1635  | [read-through cache](#rune-1635)      | authored   | generated_draft | `read-through cache` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the read-through cache obligation named before work proceeds.               |
| 1636  | [real-time {Rune}](#rune-1636)        | authored   | generated_draft | `real-time` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the real-time obligation named before work proceeds.               |
| 1637  | [rule-based](#rune-1637)              | authored   | generated_draft | `rule-based` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the rule-based obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 1638  | [side-effect-free {Rune}](#rune-1638) | authored   | generated_draft | `side-effect-free` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the side-effect-free obligation named before work proceeds. |
| 1639  | [single source of truth](#rune-1639)  | authored   | reviewed        | One canonical place others are expected to mirror.                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| 1640  | [soft delete](#rune-1640)             | authored   | generated_draft | `soft delete` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the soft delete obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 1641  | [statefulset](#rune-1641)             | authored   | generated_draft | `statefulset` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the statefulset obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 1642  | [write path](#rune-1642)              | authored   | generated_draft | `write path` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the write path obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 1643  | [write-through cache](#rune-1643)     | authored   | generated_draft | `write-through cache` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the write-through cache obligation named before work proceeds.             |
| 1644  | [zero-downtime](#rune-1644)           | authored   | reviewed        | Change introduced without taking the service dark.                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| 1645  | [zero-knowledge](#rune-1645)          | authored   | generated_draft | `zero-knowledge` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the zero-knowledge obligation named before work proceeds.                       |

## Entries

### [1583] -able {#rune-1583}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-able` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -able obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-able` at rune 1583 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-able` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-able` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -able obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-able` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-able` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1584] -aware {#rune-1584}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Marks that something notices the surrounding condition it names.

**Shadow:** misusing `-aware` at rune 1584 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-aware` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Marks that something notices the surrounding condition it names.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-aware` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-aware` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Marks that something notices the surrounding condition it names.

### [1585] -by-design {#rune-1585}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-by-design` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -by-design obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-by-design` at rune 1585 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-by-design` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-by-design` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -by-design obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-by-design` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-by-design` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1586] -centric {#rune-1586}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-centric` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -centric obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-centric` at rune 1586 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-centric` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-centric` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -centric obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-centric` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-centric` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1587] -complete {#rune-1587}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-complete` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -complete obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-complete` at rune 1587 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-complete` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-complete` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -complete obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-complete` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-complete` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1588] -driven {#rune-1588}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Signals that behavior is organized around events or causes.

**Shadow:** misusing `-driven` at rune 1588 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-driven` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Signals that behavior is organized around events or causes.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-driven` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-driven` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Signals that behavior is organized around events or causes.

### [1589] -first {#rune-1589}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Declares priority or design center: security-first, API-first, offline-first.

**Shadow:** misusing `-first` at rune 1589 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-first` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Declares priority or design center: security-first, API-first, offline-first.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-first` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-first` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Declares priority or design center: security-first, API-first, offline-first.

### [1590] -free {#rune-1590}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-free` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -free obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-free` at rune 1590 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-free` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-free` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -free obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-free` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-free` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1591] -friendly {#rune-1591}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-friendly` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -friendly obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-friendly` at rune 1591 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-friendly` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-friendly` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -friendly obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-friendly` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-friendly` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1592] -hard {#rune-1592}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-hard` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -hard obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-hard` at rune 1592 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-hard` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-hard` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -hard obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-hard` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-hard` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1593] -less {#rune-1593}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-less` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -less obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-less` at rune 1593 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-less` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-less` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -less obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-less` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-less` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1594] -local {#rune-1594}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-local` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -local obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-local` at rune 1594 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-local` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-local` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -local obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-local` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-local` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1595] -native {#rune-1595}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-native` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -native obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-native` at rune 1595 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-native` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-native` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -native obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-native` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-native` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1596] -of-record {#rune-1596}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-of-record` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -of-record obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-of-record` at rune 1596 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-of-record` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-of-record` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -of-record obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-of-record` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-of-record` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1597] -oriented {#rune-1597}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-oriented` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -oriented obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-oriented` at rune 1597 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-oriented` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-oriented` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -oriented obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-oriented` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-oriented` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1598] -safe {#rune-1598}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Asserts a protected behavior such as memory-safe or thread-safe.

**Shadow:** misusing `-safe` at rune 1598 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-safe` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Asserts a protected behavior such as memory-safe or thread-safe.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-safe` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-safe` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Asserts a protected behavior such as memory-safe or thread-safe.

### [1599] -sensitive {#rune-1599}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-sensitive` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -sensitive obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-sensitive` at rune 1599 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-sensitive` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-sensitive` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -sensitive obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-sensitive` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-sensitive` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1600] -shaped {#rune-1600}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-shaped` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -shaped obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-shaped` at rune 1600 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-shaped` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-shaped` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -shaped obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-shaped` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-shaped` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1601] -soft {#rune-1601}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-soft` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -soft obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-soft` at rune 1601 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-soft` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-soft` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -soft obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-soft` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-soft` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1602] -tolerant {#rune-1602}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-tolerant` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -tolerant obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-tolerant` at rune 1602 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-tolerant` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-tolerant` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -tolerant obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-tolerant` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-tolerant` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1603] -wide {#rune-1603}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `-wide` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -wide obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `-wide` at rune 1603 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `-wide` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `-wide` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the -wide obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `-wide` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `-wide` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1604] actor model {#rune-1604}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `actor model` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the actor model obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `actor model` at rune 1604 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `actor model` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `actor model` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the actor model obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `actor model` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `actor model` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1605] API-first {#rune-1605}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Design the interface surface before the implementations behind it.

**Shadow:** misusing `API-first` at rune 1605 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `API-first` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Design the interface surface before the implementations behind it.
- Ask the assistant to state the `API-first` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `API-first` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Design the interface surface before the implementations behind it.

### [1606] append-only {#rune-1606}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** History grows by addition rather than mutation.

**Shadow:** misusing `append-only` at rune 1606 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `append-only` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: History grows by addition rather than mutation.
- Ask the assistant to state the `append-only` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `append-only` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** History grows by addition rather than mutation.

### [1607] best-effort {#rune-1607}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `best-effort` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the best-effort obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `best-effort` at rune 1607 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `best-effort` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `best-effort` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the best-effort obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `best-effort` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `best-effort` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1608] branch-free {#rune-1608}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `branch-free` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the branch-free obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `branch-free` at rune 1608 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `branch-free` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `branch-free` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the branch-free obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `branch-free` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `branch-free` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1609] cloud-native {#rune-1609}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `cloud-native` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the cloud-native obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `cloud-native` at rune 1609 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `cloud-native` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `cloud-native` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the cloud-native obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `cloud-native` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `cloud-native` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1610] command-query {#rune-1610}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `command-query` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the command-query obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `command-query` at rune 1610 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `command-query` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `command-query` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the command-query obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `command-query` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `command-query` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1611] control plane {Rune} {#rune-1611}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `control plane` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the control plane obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `control plane` at rune 1611 in the Rune lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `control plane` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `control plane` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the control plane obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `control plane` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `control plane` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1612] copy-on-write {Rune} {#rune-1612}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `copy-on-write` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the copy-on-write obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `copy-on-write` at rune 1612 in the Rune lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `copy-on-write` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `copy-on-write` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the copy-on-write obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `copy-on-write` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `copy-on-write` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1613] crash-only {#rune-1613}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Only live briefly, start fast, and recover by restart rather than repair.

**Shadow:** misusing `crash-only` at rune 1613 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `crash-only` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Only live briefly, start fast, and recover by restart rather than repair.
- Ask the assistant to state the `crash-only` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `crash-only` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Only live briefly, start fast, and recover by restart rather than repair.

### [1614] data plane {Rune} {#rune-1614}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `data plane` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the data plane obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `data plane` at rune 1614 in the Rune lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `data plane` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `data plane` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the data plane obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `data plane` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `data plane` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1615] data-driven {#rune-1615}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `data-driven` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the data-driven obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `data-driven` at rune 1615 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `data-driven` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `data-driven` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the data-driven obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `data-driven` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `data-driven` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1616] default-deny {#rune-1616}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Access is forbidden unless explicitly granted.

**Shadow:** misusing `default-deny` at rune 1616 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `default-deny` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Access is forbidden unless explicitly granted.
- Ask the assistant to state the `default-deny` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `default-deny` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Access is forbidden unless explicitly granted.

### [1617] edge-native {#rune-1617}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `edge-native` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the edge-native obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `edge-native` at rune 1617 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `edge-native` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `edge-native` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the edge-native obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `edge-native` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `edge-native` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1618] event-driven {#rune-1618}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Action propagates from events rather than from direct polling or command loops.

**Shadow:** misusing `event-driven` at rune 1618 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `event-driven` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Action propagates from events rather than from direct polling or command loops.
- Ask the assistant to state the `event-driven` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `event-driven` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Action propagates from events rather than from direct polling or command loops.

### [1619] eventually consistent {#rune-1619}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `eventually consistent` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the eventually consistent obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `eventually consistent` at rune 1619 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `eventually consistent` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `eventually consistent` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the eventually consistent obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `eventually consistent` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `eventually consistent` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1620] fail-fast {#rune-1620}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Abort early when assumptions break instead of smearing damage forward.

**Shadow:** misusing `fail-fast` at rune 1620 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `fail-fast` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Abort early when assumptions break instead of smearing damage forward.
- Ask the assistant to state the `fail-fast` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `fail-fast` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Abort early when assumptions break instead of smearing damage forward.

### [1621] first-class {#rune-1621}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `first-class` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the first-class obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `first-class` at rune 1621 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `first-class` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `first-class` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the first-class obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `first-class` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `first-class` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1622] hot path {Rune} {#rune-1622}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `hot path` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the hot path obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `hot path` at rune 1622 in the Rune lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `hot path` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `hot path` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the hot path obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `hot path` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `hot path` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1623] infrastructure-as-code {#rune-1623}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Operational change expressed as reviewable source.

**Shadow:** misusing `infrastructure-as-code` at rune 1623 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `infrastructure-as-code` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Operational change expressed as reviewable source.
- Ask the assistant to state the `infrastructure-as-code` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `infrastructure-as-code` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Operational change expressed as reviewable source.

### [1624] least-authority {#rune-1624}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `least-authority` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the least-authority obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `least-authority` at rune 1624 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `least-authority` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `least-authority` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the least-authority obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `least-authority` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `least-authority` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1625] lock-free {Rune} {#rune-1625}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `lock-free` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the lock-free obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `lock-free` at rune 1625 in the Rune lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `lock-free` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `lock-free` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the lock-free obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `lock-free` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `lock-free` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1626] machine-readable {#rune-1626}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Structured so software, not only humans, can read it reliably.

**Shadow:** misusing `machine-readable` at rune 1626 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `machine-readable` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Structured so software, not only humans, can read it reliably.
- Ask the assistant to state the `machine-readable` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `machine-readable` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Structured so software, not only humans, can read it reliably.

### [1627] memory-safe {Rune} {#rune-1627}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `memory-safe` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the memory-safe obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `memory-safe` at rune 1627 in the Rune lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `memory-safe` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `memory-safe` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the memory-safe obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `memory-safe` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `memory-safe` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1628] multi-tenant {#rune-1628}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `multi-tenant` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the multi-tenant obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `multi-tenant` at rune 1628 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `multi-tenant` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `multi-tenant` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the multi-tenant obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `multi-tenant` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `multi-tenant` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1629] negative cache {#rune-1629}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `negative cache` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the negative cache obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `negative cache` at rune 1629 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `negative cache` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `negative cache` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the negative cache obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `negative cache` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `negative cache` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1630] object-capability {#rune-1630}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `object-capability` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the object-capability obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `object-capability` at rune 1630 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `object-capability` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `object-capability` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the object-capability obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `object-capability` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `object-capability` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1631] offline-first {Rune} {#rune-1631}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Designed to keep working with poor or absent connectivity.

**Shadow:** misusing `offline-first` at rune 1631 in the Rune lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `offline-first` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Designed to keep working with poor or absent connectivity.
- Ask the assistant to state the `offline-first` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `offline-first` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Designed to keep working with poor or absent connectivity.

### [1632] policy-as-code {#rune-1632}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Policy declared as versioned, testable code.

**Shadow:** misusing `policy-as-code` at rune 1632 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `policy-as-code` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Policy declared as versioned, testable code.
- Ask the assistant to state the `policy-as-code` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `policy-as-code` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Policy declared as versioned, testable code.

### [1633] queue-backed {#rune-1633}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `queue-backed` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the queue-backed obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `queue-backed` at rune 1633 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `queue-backed` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `queue-backed` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the queue-backed obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `queue-backed` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `queue-backed` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1634] read path {#rune-1634}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `read path` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the read path obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `read path` at rune 1634 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `read path` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `read path` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the read path obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `read path` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `read path` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1635] read-through cache {#rune-1635}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `read-through cache` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the read-through cache obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `read-through cache` at rune 1635 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `read-through cache` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `read-through cache` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the read-through cache obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `read-through cache` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `read-through cache` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1636] real-time {Rune} {#rune-1636}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `real-time` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the real-time obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `real-time` at rune 1636 in the Rune lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `real-time` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `real-time` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the real-time obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `real-time` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `real-time` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1637] rule-based {#rune-1637}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `rule-based` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the rule-based obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `rule-based` at rune 1637 in the naming lane can let the modifier can imply a guarantee the base design has not earned; verify by requiring the caster to state the base term, modifier, and exact guarantee being added.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `rule-based` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `rule-based` is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the rule-based obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `rule-based` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `rule-based` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1638] side-effect-free {Rune} {#rune-1638}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `side-effect-free` in its Rune sense is a compound naming and modifiers rune for make naming modifiers precise enough to carry engineering force; use it when the artifact needs the side-effect-free obligation named before work proceeds.
